reasoning: The base rate of the U.S. launching a full-scale military invasion to establish control over a sovereign state is extremely low, averaging roughly one such event every decade globally, and the U.S. has never invaded Venezuela. Even amidst high tensions or regional crises (such as the Venezuela-Guyana border dispute), the U.S. predominantly uses sanctions, diplomatic isolation, and occasionally covert operations or coalition defensive support, rather than direct invasions to conquer territory. Furthermore, the knowledge cutoff is late October 2025, leaving only a ~2-month window for such an event to occur before the end of the year. A military offensive of this scale would require massive logistical preparation and a troop buildup that would be highly visible and politically debated months in advance. Barring an already ongoing massive mobilization, the likelihood of a sudden unprovoked invasion materializing before December 31, 2025, is negligible. Therefore, I estimate the probability at the minimum allowed threshold of 5%.
